mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XJ 4.0 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned XJ 4.0 engine (AJ6 or AJ16) typically costs £1,500–£3,200 supply only depending on generation. Low-mileage Jaguar XJ replacement engines in the UK typically range from £3,000 to £6,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XJ 4.0 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ6 and AJ16 are widely regarded as 'the most robust and reliable engine Jaguar ever made'. Both are tough and durable engines, capable of racking up big mileages. The AJ6 is said to be slightly more prone to head gasket failure on the rear two cylinders, while the AJ16 is less prone to head gasket problems but more prone to exhaust manifold cracks.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XJ 4.0?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are upper timing chain tensioner wear (rattling on start-up or at 1,700‑1,800 rpm when hot), head gasket failure on the AJ6, water pump failure, and AJ16 ignition coil problems. With regular maintenance, these engines are exceptionally durable.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an XJ 4.0 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a tidy £3,000–£10,000 XJ, a £1,500–£3,500 recon is 15‑50% of the car's value – often worth it on well‑maintained examples. The XJ is becoming a modern classic, so well‑preserved examples may justify the investment. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my XJ's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The XJ40 (1990–1994) uses the AJ6 engine. The X300 (1994–1997) uses the AJ16 engine. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair an XJ 4.0 engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – timing chain tensioner wear (if caught early), head gasket failure, water pump failure, ignition coil issues – are repairable. However, if the timing chain has skipped or the engine has suffered major internal failure, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild.

cta.label: → Compare XJ 4.0 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Upper Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (The Defining Weakness)
problems.symptoms: Engine rattle immediately after a cold or hot start, lasting 1‑2 seconds until oil pressure builds up. The noise has been identified as relaxation of the upper (secondary) timing chain tensioner resulting in temporary chain slack. A rattle at 1,700‑1,800 rpm when hot is also common.
problems.replacementCost: £1,800–£3,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (if the chain skips and causes damage).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on start-up, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: The upper chain tensioner is a common issue on older AJ16 and AJ6 engines, as the tensioner wears and loses its ability to hold oil pressure as the oil leaks past the tensioner piston, especially when the oil is hot. This can lead to timing chain slack and, if ignored, potential engine damage. The timing chain drive to the camshafts is located at the rear of the engine, making replacement a very big job that likely requires engine removal. Higher-quality synthetic oils (5W-40, 0W-40) may reduce the scoring. Frequent oil changes are essential to prevent sludge build-up.

problems.title: Head Gasket Failure (More Common on AJ6)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, white smoke from exhaust, overheating, oil in coolant, misfire, rough running.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss or overheating occurs.

problems.recommendation: The AJ6 engine is all-alloy, and the coolant should be changed every other year to prevent corrosion. Head gasket failure is not uncommon on these engines, particularly on the rear two cylinders. The AJ16 is less prone to head gasket problems. If you notice coolant loss or overheating, have the cooling system and head gasket checked immediately.

problems.title: Water Pump Failure
problems.symptoms: Coolant leaking from the front of the engine, coolant loss, sweet smell of antifreeze, overheating, bearing noise during operation.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; catastrophic overheating can cause engine damage).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you smell antifreeze or see a coolant leak, stop driving immediately.

problems.recommendation: A revised water pump assembly was introduced on 4.0-litre engines beginning with engine number 157275. A failing water pump typically presents with coolant leaking from the front of the engine, bearing noise during operation, or engine overheating. If you notice coolant loss or a leak, have the water pump inspected immediately.

problems.title: AJ16 Ignition Coil & Driveability Issues
problems.symptoms: High idle that can only be corrected with a dealer diagnostic unit, coil failures, driveability issues, power loss when warm.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — more common on AJ16 engines.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if symptoms develop.

problems.recommendation: The AJ16 uses coil‑on‑plug ignition and can suffer from coil failures and driveability issues. High idle can only be corrected with an almost extinct dealer diagnostic unit. If you experience ignition or idle issues, find a specialist with the correct dealer diagnostic equipment. The AJ6, with its traditional distributor ignition, is generally considered more robust in this regard.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar XJ 4.0 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — cheaper than engine replacement
Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Jaguar XJ 4.0? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes
* Timing chain quiet — no rattling on cold start
* Cooling system in good condition — coolant changed every two years
* No signs of head gasket failure (AJ6) or exhaust manifold cracks (AJ16)
* A well-maintained example — the AJ6/AJ16 are tough and durable engines
avoidIf: * Any sign of timing chain rattle on start-up — the defining weakness of these engines
* Any sign of coolant loss or overheating — head gasket warning
* AJ16 with unresolved ignition or idle issues
* Poor service history — these engines are intolerant of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Jaguar XJ 4.0 is powered by the AJ6 and AJ16 engines, which are widely regarded as "the most robust and reliable engine Jaguar ever made". Both engines are tough and durable, capable of racking up big mileages. However, they have known weaknesses: the upper timing chain tensioner is the primary concern, followed by head gasket issues on the AJ6 and ignition coil problems on the AJ16. Buy on service history above all else — a well-maintained XJ 4.0 is a rewarding and increasingly collectable car; a neglected example can be expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare XJ 4.0 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar XJ 4.0 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any rattling for 1‑2 seconds? (timing chain tensioner warning)
2. Service History — Regular oil changes (every 5,000‑10,000 miles) — this is non‑negotiable
3. Timing Chain Noise — Any rattle at 1,700‑1,800 rpm when hot? (tensioner warning)
4. Coolant Loss — Any sign of coolant loss? (head gasket or water pump warning)
5. AJ16 Ignition — Any high idle or coil issues? (AJ16 specific)
6. Exhaust Manifold — Any signs of cracks? (AJ16 specific)
7. Oil Pressure — Check oil pressure when hot; these engines need good oil pressure
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
